item.title: Citi, HSBC, StanChart Send More Indonesia Profits Back Home as Prabowo Demands Rise
item.description: The three biggest foreign banks in Indonesia have shipped around $640 million of their earnings out of Southeast Asia’s largest economy since 2024 as they pare exposure amid President Prabowo Subianto’s increasingly state-focused economic policies.

item.title: China, India See Top Firms Lose Market Cap Share in AI Lag
item.description: China, India, and Hong Kong have emerged as the only major stock markets worldwide where top companies account for a smaller share of market capitalization than a year ago, underscoring their lag in the global AI race.

item.title: Animation’s AI Reckoning: Filmmakers Say They Can Make Movies for 90% Less
item.description: Some animators believe AI will make films better. Others believe it will bring costs down.

item.title: US Futures Climb on Reports Peace Talks to Resume: Markets Wrap
item.description: US equity futures climbed after reports that the US and Iran stepped back from an escalation in hostilities that had threatened the fragile ceasefire underpinning peace talks.

item.title: Oil Jumps After Tanker Hit in Middle East Flare-Up
item.description: Oil advanced after a tanker carrying Qatari crude was hit during a flare-up in attacks between the US and Iran, straining a ceasefire and hampering shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: BlueBay Sees Near-Term Risk in Japan AI Stocks Followed by Rally
item.description: RBC BlueBay Asset Management remains bullish on Japanese AI-related stocks, expecting the rally to extend into 2027, while trimming near-term risk ahead of a potential slowdown in July and August.

item.title: Chinese Spies, Smuggled Drugs Fuel Takaichi’s Security Push
item.description: Chinese spies stealing Japanese industrial secrets in boardrooms. Chip smugglers ferrying Nvidia’s prized artificial intelligence semiconductors via Japan. Drug gangs quietly slipping fentanyl across Japan’s borders to a US opioid crisis.

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: 米イランが連日の攻撃応酬、骨太方針に金融政策巡り新たな文言、アラムコのヘリ墜落、AI特需でIPOブーム、記録的な猛暑の影響

item.title: Burnham Pledges Sweeping Devolution in Bid for UK Premiership
item.description: Andy Burnham will pledge the biggest devolution of power in England in modern times in his first major speech since confirming his intention to succeed Keir Starmer as prime minister.

item.title: Fed’s Barkin Warns of High Inflation, But Sees Signs of Relief
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond President Tom Barkin warned that inflation is too high, though he sees tentative signs that price pressures may moderate soon.

item.title: New Zealand Dollar Faces Tough Third Quarter on Growth Headwinds
item.description: New Zealand’s dollar is facing a subdued third quarter as a resurgent greenback and the lingering effects of the Iran war’s energy shock drag on the domestic recovery.

item.title: Bond Heavyweights Target a Market Sweet Spot for New Warsh Era
item.description: Some of the biggest bond managers are zeroing in on one specific area of the market as the best place to ride out the early days of the Kevin Warsh era.

item.title: Williams Said to Near $5.5 Billion Deal for Momentum Midstream
item.description: Williams Cos. is in advanced talks to acquire rival natural gas pipeline operator Momentum Midstream for about $5.5 billion in what would be one of its largest deals ever, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: AI Startup Firmus to Build Indonesia Data Center With Nvidia
item.description: Firmus Technologies Pty Ltd. will build its first data center project in Indonesia as part of a partnership with US chip giant Nvidia Corp., which the Australian artificial intelligence infrastructure firm said is expected to win it as much as $30 billion in committed offtake agreements in its first six years.

item.title: How Robotaxis Are Making Headway, Glitches and All
item.description: It’s one of those technologies that were always around the corner but never arrived. Now it has, kind of...

item.title: Apple’s Sweeping Price Increases Bring Home the Costs of the AI Era
item.description: The latest on the smart glasses competition.

item.title: Leverage That Fueled US Stock Rally Becomes a Growing Concern
item.description: The leverage that helped fuel the US stock rally is now becoming an increasing source of unease.

item.title: Hawkish Fed Throws Down Challenge for Emerging-Market Bond Rally
item.description: Just when it looked like emerging-market bonds would catch a break from falling energy prices, along came Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh to spoil the party.

item.title: Austria Lobbies EU to Host Anthropic After US Access Curbs
item.description: Austria is pushing the European Union to consider hosting Anthropic PBC within its borders to counter US efforts to block foreigners from using its most advanced artificial-intelligence models.

item.title: Aramco Helicopter Crash in Ras Tanura Kills All 14 Passengers
item.description: A helicopter operated by Saudi Aramco crashed Sunday in the Saudi city of Ras Tanura, killing all 14 passengers on board, the state-owned SPA news agency reported.

item.title: Europe’s Hunt for AI Stocks Leads to Power Suppliers and Banks
item.description: Investors who want additional exposure to artificial intelligence in Europe are getting creative, searching out companies that enable the technology or stand to benefit from it as the usual suspects get more pricey.

item.title: Google Caps Meta’s Use of Gemini AI, Financial Times Reports
item.description: Google has placed limits on Meta Platforms Inc.’s use of its Gemini artificial intelligence models because it could not provide as much computing capacity as the social media company wanted, according to the Financial Times.

item.title: AI Bust Risks Ripple Effects From Growth to Credit, BIS Says
item.description: An artificial-intelligence bust, inflation and fiscal stress are among the most alarming threats to global prosperity at present, the Bank for International Settlements warned.

item.title: Japan Policy Draft Backs ‘Appropriate’ Monetary Policy
item.description: The Japanese government is expected to call for “appropriate” monetary management in its basic policy guidelines, according to a document seen by Bloomberg, in an apparent bid to dissuade the Bank of Japan from further interest rate hikes.

item.title: China Says It Can Withstand EU Trade Freeze, CCTV Account Says
item.description: China can endure a further deterioration — or even a freeze — in economic and trade ties with the European Union if talks are treated as a mere formality, according to Yuyuantantian, a social-media account affiliated with China Central Television.

item.title: S. Korea to Unveil ‘Three Mega Projects’ With Samsung, SK Group
item.description: South Korea will unveil a sweeping strategy on Monday aimed at driving the nation’s great leap forward, with Samsung Electronics and SK Group set to announce major investment plans alongside new government policy initiatives.

item.title: OpenAI Hires Uber’s Prabhjeet Singh to Lead India Operations
item.description: OpenAI appointed Uber Technologies Inc.’s former India and South Asia President Prabhjeet Singh as its managing director for the country, deepening its investment in one of its fastest-growing markets.

item.title: Sydney Home Auction Activity Weakens as Costs, Tax Changes Weigh
item.description: Sydney posted its weakest weekend home auction clearance rate in more than six years as higher interest rates and property tax changes dampen activity and weigh on prices, after years of rapid gains.

item.title: Tencent Trials TenPayGo Pay App as Foreign Visits to China Rise
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd. is testing an app aimed at helping overseas visitors navigate daily life in China, Jiemian News reported on Sunday.

item.title: Burnham’s Rise Revives Talk of War Bonds to Fund the UK Military
item.description: Britain’s soon-to-be prime minister wants an array of bold new policies to attract voters who have grown tired of a Labour government mired in indecision and political backbiting. One idea that could raise Andy Burnham’s famously thick eyebrows is the issuing of war bonds.
